key_insight: Tensorion generalizes Muon from matrices to higher-order tensors by performing constrained steepest descent with a tractable tensor-norm linear minimization oracle.
engineering_relevance: It gives practitioners a structure-aware optimizer for tensor-parameterized models that can improve stability and convergence without abandoning first-order training workflows.
method: Defines a tensor norm ball that tightly controls the tensor spectral norm while keeping the oracle computable.
Solves the LMO by reducing it to operations on adaptively selected matrix unfoldings.
Recovers Muon exactly in the matrix case, ensuring consistency with the original method.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on tensor-based computer vision tasks showing better convergence behavior and more stable gradients than Adam-based and other tensor-aware baselines in the tested settings.
watch_item: Performance depends on choosing and computing adaptive unfoldings efficiently, so implementation complexity and overhead may matter in large-scale training.
paper_title: Tensorion: A Tensor-Aware Generalization of the Muon Optimizer
